scan_table_check.description: A custom table that is directly or indirectly derived from "task" must override the configuration of the "state" column to be read-only. A task derived table implements a process. A process is a sequence of activities and the state column should represent the different phases (or states) of a process. The transitions between the states should be designed and controlled. Not every state can transit into every other state - and for each state transition there should be defined conditions and access controls. With all that in mind, allowing users to select a new state from a drop-down in a form is not good UX. Hence the state field should be read-only and state transitions should be triggered through different user interactions or automatically based on changes in data or events.
scan_table_check.name: CodeSanity - State field is read-only
scan_table_check.resolution_details: Add a dictionary override record for the derived table for the state field configuring the field to be read-only.
scan_table_check.short_description: Custom task tables set state to read-only
